paper_title: Flipped model for improving students' English speaking performance

entities.description: Primary author and correspondence from the School of Foreign Languages, Can Tho University.
Co-author from the School of Foreign Languages, Can Tho University.

entities.id: School of Foreign Languages, Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution in Vietnam where the authors are based.
The primary field of research, focusing on EFL (English as a Foreign Language) instruction.
The broader educational approach combining online digital media with traditional classroom methods.
The research problem focusing on students' ability to communicate effectively in English.
The exploration of students' attitudes, motivation, and satisfaction regarding the flipped model.
An instructional strategy where students learn content outside class and practice in class.
The research design involving experimental and control groups with pre- and post-tests.
The social networking site used to host videos and supplementary reading materials.
The average speaking performance score achieved by the group using the flipped model.
The average speaking performance score achieved by the traditional instruction group.
Statistical significance value indicating a significant difference between group performances.
Measurement of students' overall positive perception of the flipped model on a 5-point scale.
Reliability measurement for the speaking tests used in the study.
